body.title: [sprint] Inference router: introduce the model<->provider connections stack (additive)
body.intention: I intend that a model<->provider connection is a first-class queryable stack, decoupled from ModelSpec's 1:1 source_name binding, so a model can be reached on N providers at different cost/latency.
body.description:
Introduce the CONNECTIONS stack (stack 3 of 4) from D-inference-router-stack-decomposition-2026-07-08. Today a model is bound 1:1 to one provider via ModelSpec.source_name; a model-on-a-provider is the atom. A connection is a first-class edge: (model_id, provider/source_name, cost, latency/time_bucket). A model has N connections.

**Affected files:** NEW unseen_university/devices/inference/connections.py (a ConnectionsRegistry alongside SourceRegistry/ModelsRegistry); tests/inference/test_connections_stack.py. READ (do not modify yet): models_registry.py, sources.py, capability_graph.py (its (task,model) eval results are a candidate cost-data source — reuse, don't reinvent).
**Design rules:** inference-tier-not-model; docs-live-in-code; test-plan-or-why-not; OOP-first (registry class).
**Scope boundary:** ADD the connections stack ONLY. Seed it from the current ModelSpec.source_name bindings WITHOUT removing that field (removal is the cutover ticket). Do NOT touch route() or _DEFAULT_RULES. Additive — no behavior change to live dispatch.
**Test plan:** a ConnectionsRegistry holds edges; a model_id can have >1 connection; query_by_model(model_id) returns all its connections; the seeded set exactly matches the current ModelSpec source_name bindings. Red before connections.py exists -> green after.
**Rollback:** delete connections.py + its test; nothing else consumes it yet (additive), so removal is clean.
**Logging:** log connection registration + query at DEBUG (high-frequency); registry build at INFO.
**Depends on:** none (foundational).
**Observability:** the seeded connection count is logged at build; a test asserts it equals the ModelSpec binding count.
